# Decisions: Azure DevOps Pipelines
> Stack: azure-devops | Status: DEFERRED | Applicable: NO
> Deferred-Reason: CI/CD uses GitHub Actions, not Azure DevOps. DEC-135 (2 Ops/SRE approvers) and DEC-142 (blue-green) contradict DEC-R2-001 (localhost) and DEC-R2-005 (solo dev). Activate when migrating to Azure DevOps.

---

## Decided Items

| ID | Priority | Scope | Decision | Notes | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| DEC-131 | HIGH | Phase 2 (DevOps Pipelines) | Must all Azure DevOps pipelines be defined as YAML in-repo with Classic pipelines prohibited except break-glass? | Yes. YAML-only in-repo. Classic pipelines disabled; break-glass requires incident ticket, 24-hour timebox, and YAML back-port within 1 business day. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-132 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Architecture) | What reusable pipeline template strategy is required (extends/templates, central library, and version pinning rules)? | Use reusable YAML templates from a central templates repo; consuming pipelines pin template versions by tag/commit and update through reviewed pull requests. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-133 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Trigger Strategy) | What mandatory trigger model applies for CI, PR validation, path filters, and scheduled runs? | CI on main and release/*; PR validation on all protected branches; path filters per service; nightly scheduled full regression and security scans. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-134 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Branch Protection) | Which required checks and branch policies must pass before merge (build, tests, security, approvals)? | Require passing build, unit tests, and security gates; 1 CODEOWNER approval; no direct pushes; branch must be up to date before merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-135 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Approvals & Separation of Duties) | What environment approval model is required, and which roles are explicitly forbidden from self-approving production deployments? | Production requires 2 approvers from Ops/SRE and Security; requester, author, and committer cannot self-approve; emergency override must be logged and reviewed within 24 hours. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-136 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Service Connections) | Must service connections use workload identity federation only, with PAT/service-principal secrets prohibited by default? | Yes. Use workload identity federation only; PAT and client-secret service connections are prohibited by default; exceptions require security waiver with 30-day expiry. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-137 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Pipeline Secrets) | What is the mandatory secret management pattern for pipelines (Key Vault-backed variables, masking, rotation cadence, zero secret echo)? | Secrets only via Key Vault-backed variable groups; no plaintext pipeline variables; output masking required; rotate credentials every 90 days; fail pipeline on secret echo detection. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-138 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Agent Strategy) | What agent policy applies (Microsoft-hosted vs self-hosted), and what hardening/patching baseline is mandatory for self-hosted agents? | Default to Microsoft-hosted agents; self-hosted agents allowed only when justified, must be hardened, ephemeral where possible, and patched on at least a weekly cadence. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-139 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Quality Gates) | Which test gates are mandatory before deployment (unit/integration/e2e coverage thresholds and fail criteria)? | Block deployment unless unit tests pass with minimum 80% line coverage, integration tests pass for changed services, and post-deploy smoke tests pass. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-140 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Security Gates) | Which security scans are mandatory and blocking (SAST, SCA, secret scan, container scan, IaC scan)? | Blocking gates: SAST high/critical findings must be zero; SCA critical must be zero; secret scan findings must be zero; container high/critical must be zero; IaC policy violations must be zero for production. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-141 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Artifact Integrity) | What artifact controls are required (immutable versioning, provenance, signing, and SBOM publication)? | Artifacts must be immutable and content-addressable, include provenance metadata, be signed before promotion, and publish SBOM for each release artifact. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-142 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Release Strategy) | Which release pattern is mandated for production (rolling, blue-green, canary), and what rollback trigger rules are required? | Production uses blue-green deployments with automated health checks and 15-minute bake time; auto-rollback on health check failure or SLO breach; manual rollback available to on-call. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-143 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Deployment Concurrency) | What deployment lock/concurrency rules apply to prevent overlapping changes in shared environments? | Enforce one active deployment per target environment with queueing/locking; superseded non-production runs may be auto-cancelled, production runs cannot overlap. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-144 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Observability) | What telemetry/alerting baseline is required for pipeline health, failure notification, and MTTR reporting? | Capture pipeline success rate, duration, and MTTR metrics; alert on repeated failures and abnormal duration to team channel/on-call with weekly trend review. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-145 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Retention & Cleanup) | What retention policy is required for logs, test evidence, and artifacts, and what automated cleanup rules apply? | Keep release logs/test evidence for minimum 365 days, non-release artifacts for 90 days, and enforce automated cleanup jobs with exceptions for active investigations. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-146 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline DR) | What disaster-recovery expectations apply for pipeline operations (critical pipeline restore RTO, backup/export strategy, access recovery)? | Critical deployment pipelines require documented restore procedure with RTO <= 4 hours, regular backup/export of pipeline configuration, and emergency access recovery runbook. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-147 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Change Control) | What review and approval workflow is required for pipeline YAML/template changes (required reviewers, CODEOWNERS, change windows)? | Pipeline/template changes require PR with CODEOWNERS enforcement, at least two reviewers including a DevOps owner; production-impacting changes follow approved change window. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-148 | LOW | Phase 2 (Efficiency & Cost) | What controls are required to manage pipeline runtime cost and performance (caching, parallelism caps, timeout defaults)? | Enable dependency/build caching, define stage timeout defaults, cap parallelism per agent pool, and monitor cost-per-pipeline with monthly optimization review. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-149 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Audit Evidence) | What mandatory audit evidence must each release preserve (approvals, test/security reports, deployment logs), and for how long? | Preserve approvals, test results, security scan reports, and deployment logs per release for minimum 1 year in tamper-evident storage. | 2026-03-07 |
